prompt:
Three correctness bugs in the cost-security detectors:

1. @prune/cost-security tool-error-rate breaker: massive false positives. Per the tool_result contract, `is_error` is OMITTED on success and set only on failure — but the rate's denominator counts only TAGGED results, so it collapses to the error count: a session with 4 errors out of 104 calls reports a ~100% error rate. The denominator must be all results (absent tag = success); `insufficient_signal` is only for hosts that never tag anything.
2. @prune/intelligence identical-action loop detector: it hard-blocks at the threshold, but the hook that consumes it is also registered on Stop, where a block FORCES the agent to continue — the opposite of breaking a loop — and 3 repetitions can be legitimate (re-reads). It should be advisory on live tool turns only and never fire on Stop.
3. The canonical-key derivation in the same detector mis-identifies a shared acyclic sub-object (a DAG) as a cycle because the cycle guard uses a persistent seen-set instead of ancestor-only tracking, and its separator/sentinel literals contain raw NUL bytes. Distinct inputs sharing a sub-object must canonicalize distinctly, with printable-ASCII sentinels.
oracleCmd: (cd packages/cost-security && npx vitest run) && (cd packages/intelligence && npx vitest run)
notes: Reference: fix(cost-security) critical-review fixes 2026-06-07. Two-package debug task.
